User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the Cameroon Past Questions platform.

emailVerified.description: Indicates whether the user's email address has been verified.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the user's account is active.

termsAccepted.description: Indicates whether the user accepted the terms and conditions.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a past question paper document.

examType.description: The type of exam (e.g., GCE, University of Buea).

year.description: The year the question paper was used.

level.description: The level of the exam (e.g., O Level, A Level).

visibility.description: The visibility of the document (public, private).

storagePath.description: The path to the document in cloud storage.

fileSize.description: The size of the document file in bytes.

uploadedBy.description: Reference to the User who uploaded the document. (Relationship: User 1:N Document)

downloadsCount.description: The number of times the document has been downloaded.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the document was created.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Represents a payment made by a user.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the payment. (Relationship: User 1:N Payment)

providerRef.description: The payment provider's reference for the transaction.

status.description: The status of the payment (pending, confirmed, failed).

proofUrl.description: URL to the payment proof screenshot.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the payment was initiated.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the payment was last updated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the download event.

userId.description: Reference to the User who downloaded the document. (Relationship: User 1:N Download)

documentId.description: Reference to the Document that was downloaded. (Relationship: Document 1:N Download)

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the download event.

RateLimit.title: RateLimit
RateLimit.description: Represents rate limiting information for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the rate limit record.

userId.description: Reference to the User to whom the rate limit applies. (Relationship: User 1:N RateLimit)

endpoint.description: The API endpoint being rate limited.

requestsPerMinute.description: The number of requests allowed per minute.

downloadsPerDay.description: The number of downloads allowed per day.

lastRequestTime.description: The timestamp of the last request made.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Represents an audit log entry for admin actions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the audit log entry.

actorId.description: Reference to the User who performed the action (the admin). (Relationship: User 1:N AuditLog)

action.description: The action performed (e.g., user activation, document deletion).

metadata.description: Metadata associated with the action (e.g., user ID, document ID).

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification sent to a user.

userId.description: Reference to the User who received the notification. (Relationship: User 1:N Notification)

type.description: The type of notification (e.g., registration, payment received).

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the notification was sent.

startAt.description: The timestamp when the announcement starts being displayed.

endAt.description: The timestamp when the announcement stops being displayed.

Analytic.title: Analytic
Analytic.description: Represents analytics data for the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the analytics record.

date.description: The date for which the analytics data is recorded.

registrations.description: The number of registrations on that date.

paymentInitiated.description: The number of payment initiations on that date.

paymentConfirmed.description: The number of confirmed payments on that date.

documentUploaded.description: The number of documents uploaded on that date.

documentDownloaded.description: The number of documents downloaded on that date.

Config.title: Config
Config.description: Represents global configuration settings for the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the configuration record.

activationFee.description: The fee required to activate an account.

paymentProviders.description: The list of supported payment providers.

maintenanceMode.description: Indicates whether the platform is in maintenance mode.

name.description: The name of the template (e.g., registration, activation).

body.description: The HTML body of the email.

Webhook.title: Webhook
Webhook.description: Represents a webhook event received from a payment provider.

id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook event.

paymentId.description: Reference to the Payment associated with the webhook. (Relationship: Payment 1:N Webhook)

providerRef.description: The payment provider's reference for the transaction.

payload.description: The raw payload received from the payment provider.

receivedAt.description: The timestamp when the webhook was received.

AccessLog.title: AccessLog
AccessLog.description: Represents an API access log entry.

id.description: Unique identifier for the access log entry.

userId.description: Reference to the User who accessed the API. (Relationship: User 1:N AccessLog) Nullable for anonymous access.

ip.description: The IP address of the client.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the API was accessed.

ExamType.title: ExamType
ExamType.description: Represents a type of exam (e.g., GCE, University of Buea).

id.description: Unique identifier for the exam type.

name.description: The name of the exam type.

slug.description: A URL-friendly slug for the exam type.

description.description: A description of the exam type.

code.description: A short code for the subject.

aliases.description: Alternative names or synonyms for the subject.

DMCA Takedown.title: DMCA Takedown
DMCA Takedown.description: Represents a DMCA takedown request for a document.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DMCA takedown request.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document that is the subject of the takedown request. (Relationship: Document 1:N DMCA Takedown)

requesterContact.description: Contact information for the person submitting the takedown request.

reason.description: The reason for the takedown request.

status.description: The status of the takedown request (under_review, removed).

RoleAudit.title: RoleAudit
RoleAudit.description: Represents an audit log entry for role changes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the role audit entry.

adminId.description: Reference to the Admin User who made the role change. (Relationship: User 1:N RoleAudit)

targetUserId.description: Reference to the User whose role was changed. (Relationship: User 1:N RoleAudit)

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the role was changed.

PriceChange.title: PriceChange
PriceChange.description: Represents a change in the activation fee price.

id.description: Unique identifier for the price change entry.

adminId.description: Reference to the Admin User who changed the price. (Relationship: User 1:N PriceChange)

effectiveDate.description: The date when the price change became effective.

definition.description: Stores user account information. 'role' field determines user privileges.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores metadata for all publicly available documents.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document.

definition.description: Stores payment information for a user. The 'userId' field should match the userId parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who made the payment.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the payment.

definition.description: Stores download records for a user.  The 'userId' field should match the userId parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who downloaded the document.

params.name: downloadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the download event.

definition.description: Stores rate limiting information for a user. The 'userId' field should match the userId parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user to whom the rate limit applies.

params.name: rateLimitId
params.description: The unique identifier for the rate limit record.

definition.description: Stores notifications for a user.  The 'userId' field should match the userId parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who received the notification.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification.

definition.description: Stores API access logs. The 'userId' field may be null for anonymous access.

params.name: accessLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the access log entry.

definition.description: Collection to store UIDs of admin users. Existence of a document with a UID as ID grants admin privileges.  (Existence over Content)

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the admin user.

definition.description: Stores audit logs for admin actions.

params.name: auditLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the audit log entry.

params.name: announcementId
params.description: The unique identifier for the announcement.

definition.description: Stores analytics data for the platform.

params.name: analyticId
params.description: The unique identifier for the analytics record.

definition.description: Stores global configuration settings for the platform.

params.name: configId
params.description: The unique identifier for the configuration record.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the template.

definition.description: Stores webhook events received from payment providers.

params.name: webhookId
params.description: The unique identifier for the webhook event.

params.name: examTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exam type.

params.name: subjectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the subject.

params.name: dmcaTakedownId
params.description: The unique identifier for the DMCA takedown request.

params.name: roleAuditId
params.description: The unique identifier for the role audit entry.

params.name: priceChangeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the price change entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to prioritize authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. It adheres to the principles of DBAC (Database-Based Access Control) using `request.auth.uid` and avoids complex hierarchical authorization dependencies. The structure facilitates secure list operations through structural segregation and membership maps where necessary.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
*   User roles are stored directly in the `users` document (`/users/{userId}`).
*   Access to user-owned data (documents, payments, downloads, rate limits, notifications, access logs) is controlled via hierarchical paths, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. For instance, documents uploaded by a user are stored under `/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}`.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
*   The `users` collection stores user data.
*   Documents are organized under the respective user, ensuring consistent ownership. Public documents could be put in a `/documents` collection with a `visibility` field, ensuring only active users can view them. A suggested alternative is to create a separate collection `/public_documents` and `/private_documents`. Segregating them makes it clear to the user what they have access to.
*   Admin-related data (audit logs, role audits, price changes) are stored separately and can be secured using specific roles.
*   Global configuration settings and email templates are stored in dedicated collections with appropriate security restrictions.

**Access Modeling (Standardization and Consistency):**
*   User-owned data follows the `/users/{userId}/...` pattern.
*   Admin roles are managed via the `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection. A user's UID existing as a document ID in this collection grants admin privileges. This approach relies on **Existence over Content**, as recommended.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   Secure `list` operations are supported by the structure.  For example, listing a user's documents is done via a collection group query on `/users/{userId}/documents`. The security rules can easily verify `request.auth.uid == userId`.

**Invariants:**
*   Timestamps (createdAt, updatedAt) can be enforced in rules.
*   The structure supports the integrity of ownership (e.g., a document's `uploadedBy` field matches the user ID in the path).

This design ensures straightforward security rules, avoids complex queries, and enables atomic operations, leading to a robust and maintainable application.
